content:
Your LinkedIn feed this week: "I built a second brain." "How I run 12 agents at once." "10 MCP servers you need." It's 10:30AM Tuesday and that's the 3rd one I've scrolled past.

I orchestrate agents daily. Use 2-3 MCPs and 10 skills regularly. The second brain on my computer? Gathering dust for months.

Last month I bought a 5-year-old M1 Max MacBook for $1,300 for my music project SUIMA. The latest maxed-out M5 Max next to it was $7,349 with 128GB of RAM. Ten years ago I'd have gone into debt for the M5 Max and only used a third of it.

Here's what I finally learned after 15 years of terrible purchases and too much time spent researching the best of the best: you don't earn the best gear by buying it. You earn it by needing it.

Same goes for Claude.

Half my LinkedIn feed is installing new repos, new frameworks, new wrappers. Trying to make Claude better, faster, smarter. Multi-agent boilerplate before they've tried one agent. Second-brain setups on their computers, but their projects haven't shipped a feature in months. Token-optimization playbooks for context windows they've never come close to filling.

Tuning the bike shed instead of practicing how to actually ride the dang thing.

Here's the thing, installing FEELS like work. It's measurable. It's debatable. It's safer than the actual hard part: shipping features with what Claude already gives you.

So they install.

Look, I'm not saying any of this is bad. Tools matter. Eventually. When the constraint is real and you can feel it in your builds, that's when you reach for sub-agents, custom MCP servers, that dang glorious second brain.

Multi-agent workflows? I earned those by hitting Claude's single-agent ceiling so often I felt it in my sleep. The second brain? I built it before I'd hit any limit at all. It's been gathering dust ever since. An artifact of FOMO, not need.

On your own projects, have you actually hit the ceiling of what Claude can do on its own? If not, you don't have a tooling problem. You have a shipping problem.

You need to earn the right to optimize first.

Third-party tools are a reward for hitting Claude's ceiling. Not a substitute for finding it.

What's something you "optimized" way before you'd earned it?

🎛️
